You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: c0066b4a).

## Issue to Resolve

Remove default for allowed wildcard imports in `ktlint_official` codestyle
>Expected Behavior
> When java.util.* is included as a wildcard import, it should trigger the warning for no-wildcard-imports.
> ...
> Given that, I think it makes sense that ktlint should stop including those two paths as default wildcard exceptions.

_Originally posted by @greg-patterson in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1792#issuecomment-1407098815_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

The feature to respect setting ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand  was implemented not too long ago in ktlint 0.45.x via https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1272. The reason for adding the default values is that they seems to be hard-coded in IntelliJ IDEA as well (https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82).

If the property is not set explicitly, IntelliJ IDEA allows imports java.util.* and kotlinx.android.synthetic.*. If ktlint would not do the same, this would result in a conclict between ktlint and IntelliJ IDEA. Lots of users of ktlint dislike that.

In the new codestyle `ktlint_official` the default value for allowed wildcard imports shoulbe be cleared.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `.editorconfig`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `docs/rules/standard.md`
- `ktlint-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/cli/internal/KtlintCommandLine.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/RuleExecutionContext.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering.PatternEntry
  9→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 97→            )
 98→    }
 99→}
100→
101→public val NO_WILDCARD_IMPORTS_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoWildcardImportsRule().ruleId
102→

  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code

  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.RuleExecutionRuleFilter
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.RunAfterRuleFilter
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.ruleRunners
 14→import mu.KotlinLogging
 15→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.FileASTNode
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiElement
 18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiErrorElement
 19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.idea.KotlinLanguage

 21→
 22→private val KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER = KotlinPsiFileFactoryProvider()
 23→
 24→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 25→
 26→internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 27→    val code: Code,
 28→    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 29→    val ruleRunners: Set<RuleRunner>,
 30→    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 31→    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 32→) {
 33→    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 34→
 35→    init {
 36→        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 37→    }
 38→
 39→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 40→        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode)
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    fun executeRule(
 44→        rule: Rule,
 45→        fqRuleId: String,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48→    ) {
 49→        try {
 50→            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 51→            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 52→                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 53→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 54→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 55→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties),
 56→            )
 57→            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, fqRuleId, autoCorrect, emit)
 58→            rule.afterLastNode()
 59→        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 60→            throw KtLintRuleException(
 61→                e.line,
 62→                e.col,
 63→                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 64→                """
 65→                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileName}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 66→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 67→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 68→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 69→                """.trimIndent(),
 70→                e.cause,
 71→            )
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 76→        node: ASTNode,
 77→        rule: Rule,
 78→        fqRuleId: String,
 79→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        /**
 83→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 84→         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 85→         */
 86→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 87→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 88→            try {
 89→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 90→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→                }
 92→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 93→                    node
 94→                        .getChildren(null)
 95→                        .forEach { childNode ->
 96→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 97→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 98→                                    childNode,
 99→                                    rule,
100→                                    fqRuleId,
101→                                    autoCorrect,
102→                                    emit,
103→                                )
104→                            }
105→                        }
106→                }
107→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
108→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                }
110→            } catch (e: Exception) {
111→                if (autoCorrect) {
112→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
113→                    // original AST
114→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
115→                        rule,
116→                        0,
117→                        0,
118→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
119→                        e.cause ?: e,
120→                    )
121→                } else {
122→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
123→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
124→                        rule,
125→                        line,
126→                        col,
127→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
128→                        e.cause ?: e,
129→                    )
130→                }
131→            }
132→        }
133→    }
134→
135→    companion object {
136→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
137→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
138→            code: Code,
139→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
140→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine.isInvokedFromCli)
141→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
142→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
143→
144→            val psiFileName =
145→                code.fileName
146→                    ?: if (code.script) {
147→                        "File.kts"
148→                    } else {
149→                        "File.kt"
150→                    }
151→            val psiFile = psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
152→                psiFileName,
153→                KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
154→                normalizedText,
155→            ) as KtFile
156→
157→            psiFile
158→                .findErrorElement()
159→                ?.let { errorElement ->
160→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
161→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
162→                }
163→
164→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
165→
166→            val editorConfig =
167→                ktLintRuleEngine
168→                    .editorConfigLoader
169→                    .load(code.filePath)
170→                    .also {
171→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
172→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
173→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
174→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
175→                    }
176→
177→            val ruleRunners =
178→                ktLintRuleEngine.ruleRunners(
179→                    RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
180→                    RunAfterRuleFilter(),
181→                )
182→
183→            return RuleExecutionContext(
184→                code,
185→                rootNode,
186→                ruleRunners,
187→                editorConfig,
188→                positionInTextLocator,
189→            )
190→        }
191→
192→        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
193→            return text
194→                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
195→                .replace("\r", "\n")
196→                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
197→        }
198→
199→        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
200→            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
201→                return this
202→            }
203→            this.children.forEach { child ->
204→                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
205→                if (errorElement != null) {
206→                    return errorElement
207→                }
208→            }
209→            return null
210→        }
211→    }
212→}
213→
214→private fun EditorConfig.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete(
215→    propertyName: String,
216→    ktlintVersion: String,
217→) {
218→    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
219→        LOGGER.warn {
220→            "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' is obsolete and is not used by KtLint starting from version $ktlintVersion. Remove " +
221→                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
222→        }
223→    }
224→}
225→
226→private class RuleExecutionException(
227→    val rule: Rule,
228→    val line: Int,
229→    val col: Int,
230→    override val cause: Throwable,
231→) : Throwable(cause)

 81→ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 82→ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 83→ * }
 84→ * ```
 85→ */
 86→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 87→public class KtLintAssertThat(
 88→    /**
 89→     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 90→     */
 91→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
 92→    /**
 93→     * The code which is to be linted and formatted.
 94→     */
 95→    private val code: String,
 96→    /**
 97→     * Providers of rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note
 98→     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
 99→     */
100→    private val additionalRuleProviders: MutableSet<RuleProvider>,
101→) {
102→    private var filePath: String? = null
103→    private var kotlinScript = false
104→    private var editorConfigProperties = emptySet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>()
105→
106→    /**
107→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
108→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
109→     */
110→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
111→        editorConfigProperties = editorConfigProperties + properties.toSet()
112→
113→        return this
114→    }
115→
116→    /**
117→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
118→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
119→     * silently overwritten.
120→     *
121→     * Example of usage:
122→     * ```
123→     *  val code =
124→     *      """
125→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
126→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
127→     *      """.trimIndent()
128→     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
129→     *      .setMaxLineLength()
130→     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")
131→     * ```
132→     */
133→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
134→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
135→        code
136→            .split("\n")
137→            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
138→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)
139→            ?.let { index ->
140→                editorConfigProperties =
141→                    editorConfigProperties + setOf(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to (index + 1).toString())
142→            } ?: throw MissingEolMarker()
143→
144→        return this
145→    }
146→
147→    /**
148→     * Handle the code as if it was specified in file on the given path. Only to be used when the [filePath] is absolutely needed. It
149→     * results in creating a file cause a slight performance decrease of the test.
150→     */
151→    public fun asFileWithPath(filePath: String): KtLintAssertThat {
152→        this.filePath = filePath
153→        return this
154→    }
155→
156→    /**
157→     * Handle the code as Kotlin script (kts).
158→     */
159→    public fun asKotlinScript(kotlinScript: Boolean = true): KtLintAssertThat {
160→        this.kotlinScript = kotlinScript
161→        return this
162→    }
163→
164→    /**
165→     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used
166→     * to unit test rules which are best to be tested in conjunction with other rules, for example wrapping and
167→     * indenting.
168→     *
169→     * Caution:
170→     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
171→     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
172→     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
173→     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
174→     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
175→     *
176→     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProviders] when adding multiple providers of rules.
177→     */
178→    public fun addAdditionalRuleProvider(provider: () -> Rule): KtLintAssertThat {
179→        additionalRuleProviders.add(RuleProvider(provider))
180→
181→        return this
182→    }
183→
184→    /**
185→     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used
186→     * to unit test rules which are best to be tested in conjunction with other rules, for example wrapping and
187→     * indenting.
188→     *
189→     * Caution:
190→     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
191→     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
192→     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
193→     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
194→     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
195→     *
196→     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProvider] when only a singe provider of a rule is to be added.
197→     */
198→    public fun addAdditionalRuleProviders(vararg providers: (() -> Rule)): KtLintAssertThat {
199→        additionalRuleProviders.addAll(
200→            providers.map { RuleProvider(it) },
201→        )
202→
203→        return this
204→    }
205→
206→    /**
207→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s in the rule associated with the KtLintAssertThat.
208→     *
209→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
210→     */
211→    public fun hasNoLintViolations(): Unit = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolations()
212→
213→    /**
214→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for a given rule id.
215→     */
216→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
217→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId)
218→
219→    /**
220→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
221→     *
222→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
223→     */
224→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
225→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules()
226→
227→    /**
228→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] which automatically can be corrected. This is a sugar
229→     * coated version of [hasLintViolations] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
230→     */
231→    public fun hasLintViolation(
232→        line: Int,
233→        col: Int,
234→        detail: String,
235→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolation(line, col, detail)
236→
237→    /**
238→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected. Note that tests
239→     * resulting in only one (type of) [LintViolation] are usually easier to comprehend.
240→     */
241→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
242→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolations(*expectedErrors)
243→
244→    /**
245→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule which automatically can be
246→     * corrected. This is a sugar coated version of [hasLintViolations] for the case that the code contains exactly one
247→     * lint violation.
248→     */
249→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
250→        line: Int,
251→        col: Int,
252→        detail: String,
253→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(line, col, detail)
254→
255→    /**
256→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by an additional rules which can be
257→     * automatically corrected. Note that tests resulting in only one (type of) [LintViolation] are usually easier to
258→     * comprehend.
259→     */
260→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRule(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
261→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(*expectedErrors)
262→
263→    /**
264→     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
265→     */
266→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
267→
268→    /**
269→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
270→     */
271→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
272→        line: Int,
273→        col: Int,
274→        detail: String,
275→    ): Unit = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(line, col, detail)
276→
277→    /**
278→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s which can not be automatically corrected. Note that
279→     * tests resulting in only one [LintViolation] are usually easier to comprehend.
280→     */
281→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutoCorrect(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): Unit =
282→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(*expectedErrors)

284→    private fun ktLintAssertThatAssertable(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
285→        if (editorConfigProperties.isEmpty()) {
286→            KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
287→                ruleProvider = ruleProvider,
288→                code = code(),
289→                editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
290→                additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toSet(),
291→            )
292→        } else {
293→            KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
294→                ruleProvider = ruleProvider,
295→                code = code(),
296→                editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.from(*editorConfigProperties.toTypedArray()),
297→                additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toSet(),
298→            )
299→        }
300→
301→    private fun code() =
302→        if (filePath != null) {
303→            // Create a file containing the code and the given filepath. CodeFile can not be used here as it can not read successfully from
304→            // the Ktlint test file system.
305→            val path = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.resolve(filePath)
306→            KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.writeFile(path.pathString, code)
307→            Code.fromPath(path)
308→        } else {
309→            Code.fromSnippet(code, kotlinScript)
310→        }

312→    public companion object {
313→        /**
314→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
315→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules.
316→         */
317→        public fun assertThatRule(provider: () -> Rule): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat()
318→
319→        /**
320→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
321→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules. The rules provided via [additionalRuleProviders] are only
322→         * executed during the format phase of the test. This means that the unit test only has to check the lint
323→         * violations thrown by the rule for which the assertThat is created. But the code is formatted by both the rule
324→         * and the rules provided by [additionalRuleProviders] in the order as defined by the rule definitions.
325→         */
326→
327→        public fun assertThatRule(
328→            provider: () -> Rule,
329→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
330→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders)
331→
332→        private fun RuleProvider.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet()): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat =
333→            { code ->
334→                KtLintAssertThat(
335→                    ruleProvider = this,
336→                    code = code,
337→                    additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toMutableSet(),
338→                )
339→            }
340→
341→        /**
342→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
343→         */
344→        public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER: String = "Max line length marker:"
345→
346→        /**
347→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
348→         */
349→        public const val EOL_CHAR: Char = '#'
350→
351→        init {
352→            /* A unit test using the max line length marker typically looks like:
353→             * val code =
354→             *     """
355→             *     // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
356→             *     val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
357→             *     """.trimIndent()
358→             * In order for this to work properly, the length of "$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER" must be identical to the length of the real value
359→             * of the constant MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.
360→             */
361→            require("$".plus(::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name).length == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.length) {
362→                "Length of the value of the string '${'$'}MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' must be identical to length of the value of the " +
363→                    "constant. Tests using this constant will not work as intended otherwise."
364→            }
365→        }
366→    }
367→}

369→/**
370→ * Immutable assertable. Once the first assertion is made on [KtLintAssertThat] it is converted to the
371→ * [KtLintAssertThatAssertable] which allows no further modifications of the internal state. This guarantees that all
372→ * assertions operate on the same state.
373→ */
374→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
375→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
376→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
377→    private val code: Code,
378→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
379→    /**
380→     *  The rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note that
381→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
382→     */
383→    private val additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
384→) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
385→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId
386→
387→    /**
388→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the
389→     * KtLintAssertThat.
390→     *
391→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
392→     */
393→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
394→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
395→
396→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
397→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
398→
399→        assertAll(
400→            {
401→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
402→                    .describedAs(
403→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
404→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
405→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
406→                            } else {
407→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
408→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
409→                            },
410→                    )
411→                    .isEmpty()
412→            },
413→            {
414→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
415→                    .describedAs(
416→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
417→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
418→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
419→                            } else {
420→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
421→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
422→                            },
423→                    )
424→                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
425→            },
426→        )
427→    }
428→
429→    /**
430→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for the given rule id.
431→     */
432→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
433→        val (_, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
434→
435→        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })
436→            .describedAs("At least 1 lint violation was found for rule id '$ruleId' while none were expected")
437→            .isEmpty()
438→
439→        return this
440→    }
441→
442→    /**
443→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
444→     *
445→     * Note that this method can and should be chained with [isFormattedAs] to verify whether the code is correctly
446→     * formatted.
447→     *
448→     * This method can be used when the rule which is associated with the KtLintAssertThat is not violated by the sample
449→     * code, but the code is reformatted by the additional formatting rules. In case that rules are dependent on each
450→     * other, a unit test cal still verify that code is formatted correctly even when the rule under test is not
451→     * violated.
452→     */
453→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
454→        check(additionalRuleProviders.isNotEmpty()) {
455→            "hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules can only be used when additional rules have been added"
456→        }
457→
458→        val lintErrors = lint()
459→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
460→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterAdditionalRulesOnly()).isNotEmpty
461→
462→        return this
463→    }
464→
465→    /**
466→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]. This is a sugar coated version of
467→     * [hasLintViolation] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
468→     */
469→    public fun hasLintViolation(
470→        line: Int,
471→        col: Int,
472→        detail: String,
473→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
474→        hasLintViolations(
475→            LintViolation(
476→                line = line,
477→                col = col,
478→                detail = detail,
479→            ),
480→        )
481→
482→    /**
483→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule. This is a sugar coated
484→     * version of [hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
485→     */
486→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
487→        line: Int,
488→        col: Int,
489→        detail: String,
490→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
491→        hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(
492→            LintViolation(
493→                line = line,
494→                col = col,
495→                detail = detail,
496→            ),
497→        )
498→
499→    /**
500→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected.
501→     */
502→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
503→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
504→
505→        val actualLintViolationFields =
506→            lint()
507→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
508→                .toLintViolationsFields()
509→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
510→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
511→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = true))
512→        return this
513→    }
514→
515→    /**
516→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by additional rules and which can be
517→     * automatically corrected.
518→     */
519→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
520→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
521→
522→        val actualLintViolationFields =
523→            lint()
524→                .filterAdditionalRulesOnly()
525→                .toLintViolationsFields()
526→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
527→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
528→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = true))
529→        return this
530→    }
531→
532→    /**
533→     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
534→     */
535→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
536→        check(formattedCode != code.content) {
537→            "Use '.hasNoLintViolations()' instead of '.isFormattedAs(<original code>)'"
538→        }
539→
540→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
541→        val (actualFormattedCode, _) = format()
542→
543→        assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
544→            .describedAs("Code is formatted as")
545→            .isEqualTo(formattedCode)
546→
547→        return this
548→    }
549→
550→    /**
551→     * Asserts that the code contains the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
552→     */
553→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
554→        line: Int,
555→        col: Int,
556→        detail: String,
557→    ): Unit =
558→        hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(
559→            LintViolation(
560→                line = line,
561→                col = col,
562→                detail = detail,
563→            ),
564→        )
565→
566→    /**
567→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s and that those violations can not be automatically
568→     * corrected.
569→     */
570→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(vararg expectedLintViolations: LintViolation) {
571→        check(expectedLintViolations.isNotEmpty())
572→
573→        val actualLintViolationFields =
574→            lint()
575→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
576→                .toLintViolationsFields()
577→
578→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
579→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can not be automatically corrected")
580→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedLintViolations.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = false))
581→    }
582→
583→    private fun Array<out LintViolation>.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean): Array<LintViolationFields> {
584→        return map {
585→            LintViolationFields(
586→                line = it.line,
587→                col = it.col,
588→                detail = it.detail,
589→                canBeAutoCorrected = canBeAutoCorrected,
590→            )
591→        }.toTypedArray()
592→    }
593→
594→    private fun List<LintError>.filterAdditionalRulesOnly() = filter { it.ruleId != ruleId.value }
595→
596→    private fun List<LintError>.filterCurrentRuleOnly() = filter { it.ruleId == ruleId.value }
597→
598→    private fun List<LintError>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> =
599→        map {
600→            LintViolationFields(
601→                line = it.line,
602→                col = it.col,
603→                detail = it.detail,
604→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
605→            )
606→        }.toTypedArray()

608→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
609→        val ruleProviders =
610→            setOf(ruleProvider)
611→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of
612→                // the additional rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
613→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)
614→                .plus(DUMP_AST_RULE_PROVIDER)
615→        val editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride
616→            .enableExperimentalRules()
617→            .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)
618→        return KtLintRuleEngine(
619→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
620→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
621→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
622→        )
623→    }
624→
625→    private fun lint(): List<LintError> {
626→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
627→        createKtLintRuleEngine().lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
628→        return lintErrors
629→    }
630→
631→    private fun format(): Pair<String, List<LintError>> {
632→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
633→        val formattedCode = createKtLintRuleEngine().format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
634→        return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
635→    }
636→
637→    /* Representation of the field of the [LintError] that should be identical. Note that no comparison can be made
638→     * against the original [LintError] as the [canBeAutoCorrected] flag is excluded from the hashcode.
639→     */
640→    private data class LintViolationFields(
641→        val line: Int,
642→        val col: Int,
643→        val detail: String,
644→        val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean,
645→    )
646→}

648→internal class MissingEolMarker : RuntimeException(
649→    """
650→    The first line of the provide code sample should contain text '$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' which is provided by the
651→    constant '${::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name}' and ends with the EOL_CHAR'$EOL_CHAR' provided by the constant
652→    '${::EOL_CHAR.name}' which indicates the last position that is allowed.
653→    """.trimIndent(),
654→)
655→
656→/**
657→ * Expectation of the [LintError]. Contrary to the [LintError] it does not contain the ruleId. The ruleId will be
658→ * derived from the rule for which the AssertThat was created.
659→ */
660→public data class LintViolation(
661→    val line: Int,
662→    val col: Int,
663→    val detail: String,
664→)
665→
666→/**
667→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already
668→ * provided.
669→ */
670→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
671→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions = ruleProviders
672→        .asSequence()
673→        .map { ruleProvider ->
674→            ruleProvider
675→                .createNewRuleInstance()
676→                .ruleId
677→                .ruleSetId
678→                .createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty()
679→        }.distinct()
680→        .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
681→        .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
682→        .toList()
683→        .toTypedArray()
684→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
685→}
686→
687→private fun EditorConfigOverride.enableExperimentalRules(): EditorConfigOverride =
688→    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)

1→## Annotation formatting
  2→
  3→Multiple annotations should be on a separate line than the annotated declaration; annotations with parameters should each be on separate lines; annotations should be followed by a space
  4→
  5→Rule-id: `annotation`
  6→
  7→## Argument list wrapping
  8→
  9→Rule-id: `argument-list-wrapping`
 10→
 11→## Chain wrapping
 12→
 13→When wrapping chained calls `.`, `?.` and `?:` should be placed on the next line
 14→
 15→Rule id: `chain-wrapping`
 16→
 17→## Enum entry
 18→
 19→Enum entry names should be uppercase underscore-separated names.
 20→
 21→Rule id: `enum-entry-name-case`
 22→
 23→## File name
 24→
 25→Files containing only one toplevel domain should be named according to that element.
 26→
 27→Rule id: `filename`
 28→
 29→## Final newline
 30→
 31→Ensures consistent usage of a newline at the end of each file. 
 32→
 33→This rule can be configured with `.editorconfig` property [`insert_final_newline`](../configuration-ktlint/#final-newline).
 34→
 35→Rule id: `final-newline`
 36→
 37→## Import ordering
 38→
 39→Imports ordered consistently (see [Custom ktlint EditorConfig properties](#custom-ktlint-specific-editorconfig-properties) for more)
 40→
 41→Rule id: `import-ordering`
 42→
 43→## Indentation
 44→
 45→Indentation formatting - respects `.editorconfig` `indent_size` with no continuation indent (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more).
 46→
 47→Rule id: `indent`
 48→
 49→## Max line length
 50→
 51→Ensures that lines do not exceed the given length of `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more). This rule does not apply in a number of situations. For example, in the case a line exceeds the maximum line length due to and comment that disables ktlint rules than that comment is being ignored when validating the length of the line. The `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier` can be set to ignore identifiers which are enclosed in backticks, which for example is very useful when you want to allow longer names for unit tests.
 52→
 53→Rule id: `max-line-length`
 54→
 55→## Modifier order
 56→
 57→Consistent order of modifiers
 58→
 59→Rule id: `modifier-order`
 60→
 61→## Multiline if-else
 62→
 63→Braces required for multiline if/else statements.
 64→
 65→Rule id: `multiline-if-else`
 66→
 67→## No blank lines before `}`
 68→
 69→No blank lines before `}`.
 70→
 71→Rule id: `no-blank-line-before-rbrace`
 72→
 73→## No blank lines in chained method calls
 74→
 75→Rule id: `no-blank-lines-in-chained-method-calls`
 76→
 77→## No consecutive blank lines
 78→ 
 79→Rule id: `no-consecutive-blank-lines`
 80→
 81→## No empty (`{}`) class bodies
 82→
 83→Rule id: `no-empty-class-body`
 84→
 85→## No leading empty lines in method blocks
 86→ 
 87→Rule id: `no-empty-first-line-in-method-block`
 88→
 89→## No line break after else
 90→
 91→Disallows line breaks after the else keyword if that could lead to confusion, for example:
 92→```kotlin
 93→if (conditionA()) {
 94→    doSomething()
 95→} else
 96→if (conditionB()) {
 97→    doAnotherThing()
 98→}
 99→```
100→
101→Rule id: `no-line-break-after-else`
102→
103→## No line break before assignment 
104→
105→When a line is broken at an assignment (`=`) operator the break comes after the symbol.
106→
107→Rule id: `no-line-break-before-assignment`
108→
109→## No multi spaces
110→

112→
113→Rule id: `no-multi-spaces`

162→
163→Validates that the package name matches the regular expression `[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*(\.[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*)*`.
164→
165→Rule id: `package-name`
166→
167→## Parameter list wrapping
168→
169→When class/function signature doesn't fit on a single line, each parameter must be on a separate line
170→
171→Rule id: `parameter-list-wrapping`
172→
173→## Parameter wrapping
174→
175→When a function or class parameter doesn't fit on a single line, wrap the type or value to a separate line
176→
177→Rule id: `parameter-wrapping`
178→
179→## Property wrapping
180→
181→When a property doesn't fit on a single line, wrap the type or value to a separate line
182→
183→Rule id: `property-wrapping`
184→
185→## String template
186→
187→Consistent string templates (`$v` instead of `${v}`, `${p.v}` instead of `${p.v.toString()}`)
188→
189→Rule id: `string-template`
190→
191→## Trailing comma on call site
192→
193→Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on call site.
194→
195→!!! important
196→    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at call site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
197→
198→!!! note
199→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
200→    
201→    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
202→    
203→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
204→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
205→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
206→
207→!!! note
208→    Trailing comma on call site is automatically disabled if the [Wrapping](#wrapping) rule (or, before version `0.45.0`, the [Indentation](#indentation) rule) is disabled or not loaded. Because it cannot provide proper formatting with unwrapped calls. (see [dependencies](./dependencies.md)).
209→
210→    === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
211→
212→        ```kotlin
213→        FooWrapper(
214→            Foo(
215→                a = 3,
216→                b = 4,
217→            ),
218→        )
219→        ```
220→    === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
221→
222→        ```kotlin
223→        FooWrapper(Foo(
224→            a = 3,
225→            b = 4,
226→        ),) // it's weird to insert "," between unwrapped (continued) parenthesis
227→        ```
228→
229→
230→Rule id: `trailing-comma-on-call-site`
231→
232→## Trailing comma on declaration site
233→
234→Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on declaration site.
235→
236→!!! important
237→    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
238→
239→!!! note
240→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
241→
242→    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) encourages the usage of trailing comma's on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
243→    
244→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
245→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
246→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
247→
248→!!! note
249→    Trailing comma on declaration site is automatically disabled if the [Wrapping](#wrapping) rule (or, before version `0.45.0`, the [Indentation](#indentation) rule) is disabled or not loaded. Because it cannot provide proper formatting with unwrapped declarations. (see [dependencies](./dependencies.md)).
250→
251→    === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
252→
253→        ```kotlin
254→        class FooWrapper(
255→            val foo = Foo(
256→                a = 3,
257→                b = 4,
258→            ),
259→        )
260→        ```
261→    === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
262→
263→        ```kotlin
264→        class FooWrapper(val foo = Foo(
265→            a = 3,
266→            b = 4,
267→        ),) // it's weird to insert "," between unwrapped (continued) parenthesis
268→        ```
269→
270→
271→Rule id: `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site`
272→

I have implemented the fix to remove the default allowed wildcard imports in the `ktlint_official` code style.

1.  **Modified `NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`**:
    I updated the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` property definition in `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`. I added `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()` to the `EditorConfigProperty`. This ensures that when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official`, there are no allowed wildcard imports by default, while preserving the existing behavior for `intellij_idea` and `android_studio` code styles (which allow `java.util.*` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic.**` by default).

2.  **Updated Documentation**:
    I updated `docs/rules/standard.md` to reflect that the `ktlint_official` code style disallows all wildcard imports by default.

3.  **Verification**:
    I created a temporary test file `NoWildcardImportsRuleKtlintOfficialTest.kt` to verify that `java.util.*` imports are reported as violations under `ktlint_official` code style when the property is not explicitly set. The test passed. I also ran the existing `NoWildcardImportsRuleTest` to ensure no regressions for the default code style (`intellij_idea`), and those tests passed as well. I have deleted the temporary test file.

Values changed in `NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`:
```kotlin
        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
            EditorConfigProperty(
                // ...
                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList(), // Added this line
                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
            )
```

 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 97→            )
 98→    }
 99→}

 26→internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 27→    val code: Code,
 28→    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 29→    val ruleRunners: Set<RuleRunner>,
 30→    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 31→    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 32→) {
 33→    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 34→
 35→    init {
 36→        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 37→    }
 38→
 39→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 40→        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode)
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    fun executeRule(
 44→        rule: Rule,
 45→        fqRuleId: String,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48→    ) {
 49→        try {
 50→            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 51→            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 52→                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 53→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 54→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 55→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties),
 56→            )
 57→            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, fqRuleId, autoCorrect, emit)
 58→            rule.afterLastNode()
 59→        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 60→            throw KtLintRuleException(
 61→                e.line,
 62→                e.col,
 63→                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 64→                """
 65→                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileName}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 66→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 67→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 68→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 69→                """.trimIndent(),
 70→                e.cause,
 71→            )
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 76→        node: ASTNode,
 77→        rule: Rule,
 78→        fqRuleId: String,
 79→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        /**
 83→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 84→         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 85→         */
 86→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 87→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 88→            try {
 89→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 90→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→                }
 92→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 93→                    node
 94→                        .getChildren(null)
 95→                        .forEach { childNode ->
 96→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 97→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 98→                                    childNode,
 99→                                    rule,
100→                                    fqRuleId,
101→                                    autoCorrect,
102→                                    emit,
103→                                )
104→                            }
105→                        }
106→                }
107→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
108→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                }
110→            } catch (e: Exception) {
111→                if (autoCorrect) {
112→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
113→                    // original AST
114→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
115→                        rule,
116→                        0,
117→                        0,
118→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
119→                        e.cause ?: e,
120→                    )
121→                } else {
122→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
123→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
124→                        rule,
125→                        line,
126→                        col,
127→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
128→                        e.cause ?: e,
129→                    )
130→                }
131→            }
132→        }
133→    }
134→
135→    companion object {
136→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
137→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
138→            code: Code,
139→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
140→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine.isInvokedFromCli)
141→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
142→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
143→
144→            val psiFileName =
145→                code.fileName
146→                    ?: if (code.script) {
147→                        "File.kts"
148→                    } else {
149→                        "File.kt"
150→                    }
151→            val psiFile = psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
152→                psiFileName,
153→                KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
154→                normalizedText,
155→            ) as KtFile
156→
157→            psiFile
158→                .findErrorElement()
159→                ?.let { errorElement ->
160→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
161→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
162→                }
163→
164→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
165→
166→            val editorConfig =
167→                ktLintRuleEngine
168→                    .editorConfigLoader
169→                    .load(code.filePath)
170→                    .also {
171→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
172→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
173→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
174→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
175→                    }
176→
177→            val ruleRunners =
178→                ktLintRuleEngine.ruleRunners(
179→                    RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
180→                    RunAfterRuleFilter(),
181→                )
182→
183→            return RuleExecutionContext(
184→                code,
185→                rootNode,
186→                ruleRunners,
187→                editorConfig,
188→                positionInTextLocator,
189→            )
190→        }
191→
192→        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
193→            return text
194→                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
195→                .replace("\r", "\n")
196→                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
197→        }
198→
199→        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
200→            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
201→                return this
202→            }
203→            this.children.forEach { child ->
204→                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
205→                if (errorElement != null) {
206→                    return errorElement
207→                }
208→            }
209→            return null
210→        }
211→    }
212→}

214→private fun EditorConfig.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete(
215→    propertyName: String,
216→    ktlintVersion: String,
217→) {
218→    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
219→        LOGGER.warn {
220→            "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' is obsolete and is not used by KtLint starting from version $ktlintVersion. Remove " +
221→                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
222→        }
223→    }
224→}

 50→
 51→Ensures that lines do not exceed the given length of `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more). This rule does not apply in a number of situations. For example, in the case a line exceeds the maximum line length due to and comment that disables ktlint rules than that comment is being ignored when validating the length of the line. The `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier` can be set to ignore identifiers which are enclosed in backticks, which for example is very useful when you want to allow longer names for unit tests.
 52→
 53→Rule id: `max-line-length`

191→## Trailing comma on call site
192→
193→Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on call site.
194→
195→!!! important
196→    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at call site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
197→
198→!!! note
199→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
200→    
201→    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
202→    
203→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
204→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
205→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
206→
207→!!! note
208→    Trailing comma on call site is automatically disabled if the [Wrapping](#wrapping) rule (or, before version `0.45.0`, the [Indentation](#indentation) rule) is disabled or not loaded. Because it cannot provide proper formatting with unwrapped calls. (see [dependencies](./dependencies.md)).
209→
210→    === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
211→
212→        ```kotlin
213→        FooWrapper(
214→            Foo(
215→                a = 3,
216→                b = 4,
217→            ),
218→        )
219→        ```
220→    === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
221→
222→        ```kotlin
223→        FooWrapper(Foo(
224→            a = 3,
225→            b = 4,
226→        ),) // it's weird to insert "," between unwrapped (continued) parenthesis
227→        ```
228→
229→
230→Rule id: `trailing-comma-on-call-site`
231→
232→## Trailing comma on declaration site
233→
234→Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on declaration site.
235→
236→!!! important
237→    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
238→
239→!!! note
240→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
241→
242→    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) encourages the usage of trailing comma's on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
243→    
244→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
245→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
246→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
247→
248→!!! note
249→    Trailing comma on declaration site is automatically disabled if the [Wrapping](#wrapping) rule (or, before version `0.45.0`, the [Indentation](#indentation) rule) is disabled or not loaded. Because it cannot provide proper formatting with unwrapped declarations. (see [dependencies](./dependencies.md)).
250→
251→    === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
252→
253→        ```kotlin
254→        class FooWrapper(
255→            val foo = Foo(
256→                a = 3,
257→                b = 4,
258→            ),
259→        )
260→        ```
261→    === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
262→
263→        ```kotlin
264→        class FooWrapper(val foo = Foo(
265→            a = 3,
266→            b = 4,
267→        ),) // it's weird to insert "," between unwrapped (continued) parenthesis
268→        ```
269→
270→
271→Rule id: `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site`
